p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, trailing habit.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the 5-day sequence with no significant leaf drop or growth spurts.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Healthy; consistent turgor pressure and leaf color.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, central position, two primary leaves.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Showed signs of wilting in T-3 and T-2, followed by a slight recovery in T-1 and current state.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed; leaf margins show slight chlorosis, likely due to moisture fluctuations.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit scale anchor.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The apical leaf has maintained a consistent angle relative to the rabbit; no significant growth observed.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable; no signs of necrosis or yellowing.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot, shared with p2, small sprout near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remained dormant throughout the observation period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Stable; no visible growth or decline.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil in p2/p4 has transitioned from dry to showing white crystalline deposits (likely mineral salts or fertilizer residue) starting at T-1.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: White granular debris appeared on the soil surface of p2/p4 between T-2 and T-1, persisting to current.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The most significant change occurred between T-2 and T-1, where white granular material was introduced to the p2/p4 pot surface.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: Overall biome health is moderate. The introduction of white granular material in the p2/p4 pot is the primary concern, potentially indicating over-fertilization or hard water mineral buildup.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of white granular debris in p2/p4 pot; p2 leaf margin chlorosis.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is largely stable. The String of Nickels (p1) is thriving. The Pothos (p3) is static but healthy. The Mexican Mint (p2) and Silver Guest (p4) are experiencing minor stress, evidenced by leaf margin discoloration and the appearance of unexplained white granular deposits on the soil surface.
